data.title: Apimeds Faces NYSE Non-Compliance Over Delayed 10-K Filing
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Apimeds Pharmaceuticals US, Inc. ( (APUS) ) is now available.

On April 17, 2026, Apimeds Pharmaceuticals US, Inc. received a non-compliance notice from NYSE Regulation after failing to timely file its Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended Dec. 31, 2025, triggering application of the exchange’s late-filer rules. The company attributed the delay to ongoing preparation of fourth-quarter and full-year financial statements and additional audit work, and had previously flagged these issues in a March 31, 2026 Form 12b-25.

Under the NYSE American’s framework, Apimeds has an initial cure period running to Oct. 15, 2026 to file the overdue Form 10-K, during which the exchange will monitor its reporting status and any subsequent delayed filings. The company’s stock will remain listed under APUS with a “.LF” late-filer designation, while an April 2, 2026 trading halt remains in effect and the exchange retains broad discretion to truncate the cure period and move to suspend or delist the shares if reporting delays persist or continued listing is deemed inadvisable.

More about Apimeds Pharmaceuticals US, Inc.

Apimeds Pharmaceuticals US, Inc. operates in the pharmaceutical industry and is listed on the NYSE American under the ticker symbol APUS. The company focuses on developing and commercializing drug products, positioning itself within the U.S. healthcare and life sciences market as a publicly traded pharmaceutical issuer subject to SEC reporting and exchange compliance obligations.

Average Trading Volume: 182,622

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $23.64M

Learn more about APUS stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Getty Realty Raises 2026 AFFO Guidance on Strong Growth
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Getty Realty ( (GTY) ) has provided an announcement.

Getty Realty Corp., a REIT specializing in freestanding convenience and automotive retail real estate such as convenience stores, car washes, auto service centers and drive-thru QSRs, reported that as of March 31, 2026 it controlled 1,191 properties across 45 states with $225 million in annualized base rent, 99.7% occupancy and tenant rent coverage of 2.5 times. The company’s tenant roster skews toward multi-store national and regional brands under predominantly unitary triple-net leases, and it has diversified meaningfully by property type and geography since 2019 while maintaining an investment-grade balance sheet and more than $625 million of total liquidity.

In a corporate presentation furnished for investors beginning April 22, 2026, Getty detailed that it invested $34.4 million year-to-date at an 8.0% initial cash yield, acquiring 17 auto service centers and six drive-thru QSRs, and has more than $125 million of additional investments under contract expected to close over the next 12 months. For the first quarter of 2026, AFFO rose 15.3% to $39.0 million and AFFO per share increased 6.8% to $0.63, prompting management to raise full-year 2026 AFFO guidance to a range of $2.50 to $2.52 per share, signaling confidence in accelerated growth supported by strong rent collections, long lease terms and no debt maturities until June 2028.

The most recent analyst rating on (GTY) stock is a Buy with a $37.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Getty Realty stock, see the GTY Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GTY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GTY is a Outperform.

The score is driven mainly by solid financial performance (profitability and cash flow strength tempered by higher leverage) and a positive earnings outlook with strong portfolio metrics. Technicals support the uptrend but are overbought, and valuation is mixed (high yield but a higher P/E), keeping the overall score in the mid-70s.

To see Spark’s full report on GTY stock, click here.

More about Getty Realty

Getty Realty Corp. is a real estate investment trust focused on freestanding convenience and automotive retail properties across the U.S., including convenience stores, express tunnel car washes, auto service centers and drive-thru quick-service restaurants. As of March 31, 2026, the company owned 1,191 properties in 45 states, generated $225 million in annualized base rent, maintained 99.7% occupancy, and serves primarily multi-store operators, national and regional brands with unitary triple-net leases and strong rent coverage.

The portfolio is diversified by property type, geography and tenant base, with more than 90% of transactions sourced directly with tenants and 85% of rent from unitary leases. Getty emphasizes durable, convenience- and service-oriented retail business models, uses detailed site-level and tenant credit underwriting, and operates with investment-grade metrics, including a BBB- rating, moderate leverage and significant liquidity to support continued external growth and redevelopment activity.

data.title: Perrigo Updates Segment Reporting and Profitability Metrics
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Perrigo Company ( (PRGO) ) has issued an announcement.

On February 26, 2026, Perrigo said it has overhauled its segment reporting to align with its product categories rather than geography, effective from the first quarter of 2026. The group now reports Self Care, Specialty Care and Infant Formula as core segments, with Oral Care and Dermacosmetics folded into an “All Other” bucket, mirroring how top management allocates resources and manages growth and profitability.

The company’s chief decision maker also shifted the primary profitability metric from segment operating income to segment adjusted operating income, though Perrigo stressed the changes do not alter historical consolidated financial position, results or cash flows. To aid comparability for investors, Perrigo has provided unaudited recast segment financial data for each quarter from March 2024 through December 2025, noting that some amounts may differ from previously disclosed figures due to redefined categories and updated allocation methods.

The most recent analyst rating on (PRGO) stock is a Hold with a $15.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Perrigo Company stock, see the PRGO Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PRGO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PRGO is a Neutral.

PRGO scores weak-to-midrange primarily due to deteriorating profitability and declining revenue trends, despite positive cash generation and reduced balance-sheet leverage. Technicals are strongly bearish with the stock trading below key moving averages and negative momentum indicators. Guidance suggests a challenging 2026 transition year with notable near-term EPS headwinds and impairment risk, while the very high dividend yield and extended revolver maturity provide partial support.

To see Spark’s full report on PRGO stock, click here.

More about Perrigo Company

Perrigo Company plc is a consumer self-care and healthcare products maker, focused on over‑the‑counter medicines, wellness items and infant nutrition. Its portfolio spans pain and sleep remedies, upper respiratory and digestive health products, vitamins and supplements, oral electrolyte beverages, women’s and skin health brands, dermacosmetics and infant formula, sold primarily to consumers seeking self‑treatment solutions.

data.title: Maze Therapeutics prices $150 million equity offering
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Maze Therapeutics, Inc. ( (MAZE) ).

On April 21, 2026, Maze Therapeutics, Inc. priced an underwritten registered offering of 5,540,000 shares of common stock at $23.50 per share and pre-funded warrants for 850,000 shares at $23.499, for expected gross proceeds of $150 million and estimated net proceeds of about $144.7 million. The deal, underwritten solely by Leerink Partners and expected to close around April 23, 2026, drew participation from a mix of new and existing specialist healthcare and institutional investors.

Maze plans to use the proceeds primarily to advance clinical development of its lead candidates MZE829 for APOL1-mediated kidney disease and MZE782 for phenylketonuria and chronic kidney disease, as well as for general corporate purposes. The capital raise, combined with existing cash and marketable securities, is expected to fund the company’s operations into 2029, extending its cash runway and supporting its position in the competitive kidney and metabolic disease therapeutics market.

The most recent analyst rating on (MAZE) stock is a Buy with a $64.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Maze Therapeutics, Inc. stock, see the MAZE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MAZE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MAZE is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by volatile fundamentals (sharp swing from profitable 2024 to zero-revenue, high-loss, cash-burning 2025) and weak technical momentum (below key moving averages with negative MACD). Offsetting factors include constructive corporate developments (positive Phase 2 data, milestone payment, and improved liquidity options), but valuation support is limited given losses and no dividend.

To see Spark’s full report on MAZE stock, click here.

More about Maze Therapeutics, Inc.

Maze Therapeutics, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing novel small molecule precision medicines for kidney and metabolic diseases, guided by its human genetics-based Compass platform. Its pipeline is led by MZE829, a dual-mechanism APOL1 inhibitor in Phase 2 for APOL1-mediated kidney disease, and MZE782, an SLC6A19 inhibitor advancing to Phase 2 for phenylketonuria and chronic kidney disease, with headquarters in South San Francisco.

The company focuses on genetically validated targets and aims to advance first- or best-in-class programs for patients with serious renal and metabolic conditions. By leveraging variant discovery and functionalization, Maze seeks to translate human genetic insights into therapeutics, positioning itself within the precision medicine segment of the biopharma industry.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Hexcel ( (HXL) ) has provided an announcement.

Hexcel on April 22, 2026 reported strong first‑quarter 2026 results, with net sales rising 9.9% year over year to about $502 million and adjusted diluted EPS jumping to $0.59 from $0.37, driven by higher commercial aerospace build rates and improved operating leverage. Commercial aerospace sales surged 18.8% on broad program strength, while Defense, Space & Other declined after a prior divestiture, gross margin expanded to 26.9%, free cash outflow narrowed sharply, and the company refinanced its $750 million revolving credit facility to 2031, declared a $0.18 dividend, and reaffirmed its full‑year 2026 guidance despite geopolitical and supply chain headwinds.

The company’s first‑quarter performance underscored the benefits of growing back into existing capacity and maintaining operational discipline as it supports customer rate ramps, even as it absorbs restructuring costs tied to shutting industrial manufacturing in Leicester, U.K. Management highlighted that higher oil prices are reinforcing demand for lightweight composites that improve fuel efficiency, supporting Hexcel’s strategic positioning as airlines and aerospace OEMs prioritize efficiency gains, while the stronger balance sheet and unchanged outlook signal confidence to investors and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HXL) stock is a Buy with a $95.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hexcel stock, see the HXL Stock Forecast page.

Quince Therapeutics ( (QNCX) ) has shared an update.

On April 22, 2026, Quince Therapeutics increased the size of its board of directors to four members and appointed June Bray as an independent Class I director, effective immediately, to serve until the 2026 annual meeting of stockholders. Bray, who has no disclosed family or material transactional ties to the company, was also named to the Nominating & Governance, Audit, and Compensation committees, and will receive standard cash retainers under the company’s outside director compensation policy without an accompanying stock option grant.

Under this policy, Bray is entitled to a $38,000 annual retainer for board service, plus additional annual retainers of $7,500, $5,500, and $4,000 for her roles on the Nominating & Governance, Audit, and Compensation committees, respectively. She will also enter into Quince’s standard indemnification agreement for directors, reinforcing the company’s governance framework as it adds experienced independent oversight to its board and key committees.

data.title: Trio Petroleum Extends Deadline for Utah Asset Acquisition
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Trio Petroleum Corp. ( (TPET) ) has shared an update.

On May 20, 2025, Trio Petroleum Corp. disclosed that it had entered a non-binding letter of intent with Heavy Sweet Oil LLC to acquire all of HSO’s rights in 2,000 acres at the P.R. Spring area in Utah’s Uinta Basin, contingent on sustained output from two Trio-operated wells at the nearby Asphalt Ridge site. The letter of intent required each well to produce at least 40 barrels per day for 30 consecutive days by May 15, 2026, but as that benchmark has not been met and is not expected to be achieved by the initial deadline, the parties signed an extension on April 20, 2026, pushing the production test period out to May 15, 2028, preserving the potential transaction and signaling Trio’s continuing interest in the Utah assets despite operational delays.

The extension gives Trio more time to prove consistent production performance at Asphalt Ridge, a key condition for advancing to definitive agreements for the P.R. Spring acquisition and potentially expanding its footprint in the Uinta Basin. For stakeholders, the move indicates that while near-term production targets have slipped, the strategic rationale for the deal remains intact and the company is keeping optionality on a sizeable heavy oil asset package that could bolster future output if technical and operational hurdles are resolved.

More about Trio Petroleum Corp.

Trio Petroleum Corp. is an oil and gas company focused on acquiring and developing hydrocarbon assets, including heavy oil properties in regions such as Utah’s Uinta Basin. The company pursues growth through strategic transactions that expand its acreage position and potential production base in established resource plays.

Average Trading Volume: 37,170,065

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $16.55M

Third Coast Bancshares reported its first-quarter 2026 results on April 22, 2026, highlighting a completed merger with Keystone Bancshares on February 1 that added roughly $1 billion in assets, $812 million in loans and $844.2 million in deposits. The deal drove gross loans up 19.5% sequentially to $5.25 billion and deposits up 23.5% to $5.72 billion, while book value per share rose and tangible book value dipped modestly.

data.title: United Rentals Posts Record Q1 Results, Raises 2026 Outlook
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

United Rentals ( (URI) ) has provided an announcement.

United Rentals reported that on April 22, 2026, it delivered record first-quarter results, with total revenue of $3.985 billion, rental revenue of $3.419 billion, net income of $531 million and first‑quarter records in EPS and adjusted EBITDA. The company highlighted 2.3% fleet productivity growth, strong cash generation with $1.514 billion from operating activities and $1.054 billion in free cash flow, and a net leverage ratio of 1.9x alongside total liquidity of $3.377 billion at March 31, 2026.

Management cited healthy growth across both general rentals and specialty rentals, including an 8.7% year‑over‑year increase in rental revenue and double‑digit specialty revenue growth, while acknowledging margin pressure in specialty from higher depreciation, delivery costs and revenue mix. United Rentals raised its full‑year 2026 guidance for total revenue, adjusted EBITDA, net rental capital expenditures and operating cash flow, signaling confidence in demand from large projects and key verticals and underscoring its intent to sustain industry‑leading growth, profitability and shareholder returns despite ongoing restructuring-related charges and branch consolidations.

Endeavour Silver has filed a Form 6-K for April 2026 and notified investors that its annual general meeting of shareholders will be held on June 2, 2026, in Vancouver. Horizon Bancorp, Inc. on April 22, 2026 reported unaudited first quarter 2026 results that underscored resilient profitability, with annualized return on average assets of 1.62%, return on average tangible common equity of 19.02% and net interest margin of 4.29%, while net income reached $26.2 million, or $0.51 per diluted share, slightly below the prior quarter but above the year-ago core run rate. The quarter featured robust core deposit growth of 11.3% annualized, driven by 22.8% annualized growth in non-interest-bearing deposits, modest 4.0% annualized commercial loan growth, strong credit quality with net charge-offs at 0.05% of average loans, and tightly managed expenses at $40.7 million, reinforcing management’s message of stable performance and solid capital generation despite a volatile macroeconomic backdrop.

Horizon’s balance sheet metrics highlighted ample funding for future loan growth, as elevated interest-earning cash balances from strong deposit inflows modestly compressed net interest margin by about four basis points in the quarter, reflecting a strategic decision to preserve pricing discipline rather than chase lower-yielding residential mortgages. With book value per share continuing to advance and core profitability remaining in the top tier of peers, the results suggested the bank is sustaining its competitive positioning in community banking while maintaining flexibility to support disciplined asset growth and long-term shareholder returns.

data.title: Pathward Financial Posts Solid Q2 Results Amid Tax Strength
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Pathward Financial ( (CASH) ) has provided an announcement.

Pathward Financial, Inc., a U.S.-based financial holding company focused on tax services, partner solutions and commercial finance, reported fiscal second-quarter 2026 net income of $72.9 million, or $3.35 per share, for the three months ended March 31, 2026, slightly down from $75.0 million a year earlier. Management highlighted strong tax-season performance, robust partner pipeline and growing commercial finance net interest income, while Pathward, N.A. again earned Great Place To Work certification, underscoring its emphasis on culture.

For the 2026 fiscal second quarter, total revenue rose to $276.3 million, driven by a 9% increase in noninterest income, with notable gains in card and deposit fees, tax-related fee income and refund transfer product fees, which together made noninterest income 55% of revenue. New loan originations excluding tax services climbed to $1.31 billion, supported by a 2025 consumer finance contract, and the company posted a 3.56% annualized return on average assets and 54.41% return on average tangible equity.

During the six months ended March 31, 2026, tax-services product revenue increased 13% to $95.7 million, with higher refund advances and transfers and a $4.4 million reduction in credit-loss provisions, lifting net tax-services product income 30% to $62.0 million; refund advance originations reached $1.87 billion versus $1.66 billion in the 2025 season. These gains reflect efforts to grow tax products, capture market share and refine underwriting models and data analytics in this core business line.

Net interest income for the quarter fell 8% to $125.1 million, mainly due to lower income from the consumer finance portfolio and cash, following the October 2025 sale of a consumer portfolio that had carried higher reported yields, while commercial finance loans and leases generated an $8.4 million interest-income increase. Average interest-earning assets slipped to $7.65 billion as the balance sheet shifted away from cash and securities toward loans and leases, and net interest margin narrowed to 6.63%, or 5.32% on an adjusted basis excluding gross consumer finance yields, with funding costs remaining low.

Noninterest income rose 9% to $151.2 million, aided by stronger tax-related fees, card and deposit fees and refund transfer revenues, partially offset by weaker secondary market and rental income after a prior-year working-capital portfolio gain. Servicing fees on custodial deposits at partner banks increased both sequentially and year over year on higher average balances, underscoring the importance of Pathward’s partner-bank arrangements and deposit-driven revenue streams.

Noninterest expense declined 3% to $143.5 million, reflecting lower card processing and other costs, even as the company increased spending on compensation, benefits, buildings and software to support its long-term strategy. Card processing expense, which is tied to rate-indexed partner agreements and federal funds rate moves, remained significant but decreased year over year, and the company’s effective tax rate for the quarter was 16.2% on income tax expense of $14.2 million, reinforcing solid bottom-line profitability despite portfolio repositioning.

The most recent analyst rating on (CASH) stock is a Buy with a $107.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Pathward Financial stock, see the CASH Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CASH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CASH is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong underlying fundamentals (high profitability and strong returns with currently low leverage) and positive technical momentum with the stock trading above key moving averages. A raised FY2026 EPS guide and improving operating metrics further support the outlook, while historical free-cash-flow inconsistency and execution/timing risks (partner ramp and tax season) temper the score.

To see Spark’s full report on CASH stock, click here.

More about Pathward Financial

Pathward Financial, Inc., listed on Nasdaq as CASH, is a U.S.-based financial holding company focused on powering financial inclusion through a mix of tax services, commercial and consumer finance, card and deposit solutions, and partner-driven banking platforms. Its subsidiary Pathward, N.A. operates as a nationally chartered bank, and the group emphasizes partner solutions, higher-returning assets, and workplace culture, having been Certified by Great Place To Work for four consecutive years.
